_comment: Shipped model parameters. Source: football-model-lab Track C, Part 0 — the POOLED BL1+BL2 fit (not Track A's BL1-only figures), per brief §2. Refit once before each season, never during. The BL2 fields are additive deltas on the pooled values; the application rule lives in packages/engine/src/model.mjs and is taken verbatim from the lab.

provenance.weighting: equal — recency weighting did not earn its keep on held-out evidence
provenance.note: The 15-season window is the window on which the lab's recency conclusion was established. Changing the window rule is a Process B change (§5.5) and requires re-running the recency test first.
provenance._hyperComment: Whether a summer is Process A or Process B is decided by the pinned lab commit AND this hyperparameter set. Any difference means Process B (§5.5).

perLeagueFields._comment: §11 gate: does the Track C artefact distinguish league baselines? Yes — these three deltas were kept because they earned their keep on held-out evidence. BL2 therefore does NOT inherit the pooled baseline. See docs/verification/track-c-parameter.md.

ghostWindow._comment: COVID closed-door window as defined by the lab. HOME_ADV_GHOST applies only to matches inside it — relevant for V2 historical replays, inactive for the current season. §8: the pooled 95% interval [-50.8, +10.5] includes zero, so no causal claim is made anywhere in the UI.

liveRatings._comment: The one positive lab result (§2): live per-match ratings beat pre-season ratings. Season-clustered CI on log-loss. The lab's per-match intervals are deliberately NOT reproduced here — they are too narrow and §11 forbids quoting them.
